"**Texas foster care lawsuit appealed to Supreme Court** ,” Texas Tribune's **Eleanor Klibanoff** \-- "A long-running lawsuit against Texas’ foster care system may get another look after lawyers representing children in state care asked the U.S. Supreme Court to intervene.

The petition, filed Monday, asks the high court to review [a recent ruling](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eCyDm7YZItPaA-BvJlf4tsBhU9Dy-ZF_Es7KCCnsgDLbrYV2w1QeGNsbKbJO0PSPlK_k9KYRdbHSCjVRqsqZLHZ7oVD6Uhm39_7RNugjFQiiGi5LYwmA21wVgTnFAmPNmlLwD8YovP8Fm-tfzNXMZrsmOBV1ZuThXeXj7cknG7E94Sl15DFTzRqNy4GjiUpdOsncgNGANBFzMpar42jvssz49log9CpjUeZBAZvvZRWgZRi6gAUr2Wv1rk-b-xSC3eqMcNAtwgNTy1odImbm3eX8op0EdoxtY3CnDBRIpJRZTbfSORJftNXnnP9Y6IyB8hOcLU8Lrm0ZVJsKmkQCf1h6poTEMTxnDGUu3NzstfRFS635sIztQtu7EAsxl4puyecMLnhRE3T45LWVP0Sdm6ksvAUbYjgOVE53v1bd_Z8ejGhbFYINfws47wnjBSHGHb-ozHf-CJAWD2n22meY24267yYSJm-mCXsdV3-62yS_cCGCpdDR5oMPP6fx8hbuBt1u6DFQ9Yyw6sAv0EXvjKU/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h27/h001.7KObeF8bvWLyb6m_h9U-Au6_0FP6BfVoJE61HogVjLw) from the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals that overturned a contempt order against the state and removed the judge who had overseen the case to this point.

U.S. District Judge**Janis Jack** was removed from the case in October, after more than 13 years as the state’s [de facto foster care czar](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eCyDm7YZItPaA-BvJlf4tsB2kdd0YHNwDIRPfL8J0zSF6bSQKJPTF4SzdGfOZ_hpNu64NEoX8C25eQABmlgUpoCRh8hOeIn-uU6nU8is04kR2_8aCZlY35ZUS60x3ouyaFNgO54VGCldn-Kfpl2i9zUQxjoWHF3K_mVvYoN2JYFTQ5C7c6lwsDL7TsdCojetJ2jIDyed4Tb0BOu5303aWdrJRQLGcYsFUhdm2zBnb-ft031y0ccKLXPv2yAQKgF77IKNacotoYZsDf7Ax1-uc1o6V1TClKS9T_8i2MFyO8gFW0MGKWKqIGa2ySm7HdfaokrJUbrY099JESF6IZSAsm-AMJc_tElnfnWU5JrG9ZWwDC0pPkH7pCOh228i34o69VquR2zBLJOrw4IaJ0Rp1rANQxBJX-1LhDEb4ke_xlLK_8FIpU1X07wk6smBfkpjTcLI_HSo3RU7XMPzq5UlfluSmsf8IK8T1XK5bnHjorSxtV37FqLHf2R6RaIlemCgUA/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h28/h001.UWI88jdnC_MfSNlyR8fKqCqH3qjDMoSBMekNRIyKzWY). She issued the original ruling in the 2011 lawsuit that found kids were leaving state care more damaged than when they entered, after being “shuttled throughout a system where rape, abuse, psychotropic medication, and instability are the norm.”

She placed the state system under federal oversight, which the state fought in court for many years.

In the years since that initial ruling, Jack found the state in contempt three times for failing to comply with court ordered reforms. The most recent contempt order was in April 2024, when she ordered the state to pay $100,000 a day until it could prove it was properly investigating abuse and neglect allegations among the most disabled children in state care. The state appealed that ruling.

In October, the 5th Circuit [reversed](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eH94VzLqEqDPtmuAKfWRCNr8tEC5b8cFrHUkrznFg2E3Wp1E2FZEHZn1OzMJOSNQTtLwTkHje4TtZggeI6NWDTjR1gMiz6TGWmUJQ9KYTVCrVty9BkaauaBunoGfj07npzkT0VXnCBEG-mfwXD78uf9pidsL7YyFMLndvXl7xfLWqubi4i1Or6JgvZdrZHl23kZX60S636OQUcWOxy-yNJnWB78E7Zh28rhZhH0N_LloVYCAahLVMKj0P0L9pYEwF9iRYTA5Zcwdbrtf9ja1WWY3IgnAzbj2ivfMnMZi8xO4SGGhWfEdvfzhx99dUllsTgCTiayZ0nZH6dqAtofn24H6SK-qpbAmDjzmGzoSbUnLn2G-VWfkbxoGcxKzgzkD-pjPOZ8IoHRaWHXIh-MNzEYSM1xn4oS_My5l0ZEnt6wNx9TbiDTAhAtpcR3XZU_DUYkXhAjiCImXIVbeB8NApbrn7Qi1YuGMGNeBGJY8EHQvd19ujK1kvZsxD7wmR15l2A/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h29/h001.aPkbuODss8j0eT5NgHQ1PlTdfpl5NCBy00XfpY9x0pQ) Jack’s contempt order, ruling that it was criminal, not civil in nature, and ordered her removed from the case. Jack has been replaced by U.S. Chief District Judge **Randy Crane**." [Texas Tribune](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eCyDm7YZItPaA-BvJlf4tsCp9gPKRlKnYLuSTx7ymHCbDZAzrfv_8FqyZVJBFTkzJ0HLoCBwYx_b8Ix-MCdqqqstIRROGK5y7wpGkwzvNHTFxMCVKBCi7aSmDVcsLnH7Uv32CFTOXqREyFIXpy-nrgc-FFN60EZQO4ZnnvAvWreVxfuInU5D28zdlBFilybhUizGGPcRbIEFb_C7Z1mp5uKmvT3l0JCwt4xeEium79LNAKxsfhPCPxop-BcBmEYYvJZH1sxa7Oa_XMp97DBAd_2Mi_5W_QgxtPqiqGp04yO8j3-QmozLZlOEds4_azxVrVYTp0m6OfEY8PSmwPfk0mmtYJ1-MnuLedPdlO5NVdwpGKLGmlWdb9KV58xrLhEGctVEjqhWyJO8gPo9bLanpiKAAEsYdVNWVIH62wTMTm-KITffZ1qe0w42aIFDVJePeJHZvRkHGHsMcVs5e0t8oNARj0BR6g4K2VBaxuF74gNy4__k9Rj4--enagyLWsteVb_KECkDD_hSj9amRGHE3mQ/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h30/h001.USOgOP0Ls9a0WmhE5TAJyM-mJhyPKNmfxH3iq6LkjUY)

"**Ken Paxton hit with two lawsuits over county oversight rule** ,” Texas Tribune's **Ayden Runnels** \-- "Five Texas district attorneys are suing Attorney General **Ken Paxton** in two separate lawsuits filed Friday over new rules that would give the state’s top law enforcer meticulous access to their office’s records.

The two lawsuits, filed in Travis County District Court, seek to overturn a new rule created by Paxton’s office giving the attorney general office’s employees discretion to request almost all documents from cases county officials work on, regardless of whether they are being pursued. The district attorneys suing Paxton said the rule is an unconstitutional overreach that would needlessly burden offices who would have to present “terabytes” of data to the attorney general.

The rule, which took effect in April, only applies to counties with 400,000 residents or more — a threshold only 13 counties in the state meets. Paxton’s office has marked the provision as a way to “rein in rogue district attorneys” refusing to uphold the law. District attorneys from Travis and El Paso counties filed one suit, while district attorneys from Harris, Dallas and Bexar counties filed another. Both seek to block Paxton from being able to enforce the rule, alleging it violates the state constitution and federal law.

**The background:** The rule, known as Chapter 56, was originally proposed in the administrative code in September 2024. It requires district attorneys to provide all documents or communications produced or received by district attorneys’ offices, including confidential information.

Included in the rule’s definition of “case file” materials eligible for review are all documents, correspondence and handwritten notes relevant to a case. It also requires counties to submit quarterly reports to the attorney general on [twelve different](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.5lwqIBNPgUpXckHvDM7-mN1mDHHYS1V3usTSAD3dPxqb89EX3hpHFHyO0wVc5_LLYCQVT4vN3z3dbaADs08-GJPKZhrE1hej9qJ-Kv3GSJO5FVxjNr3vUnl3FPan-3yZ9phKmhSPnlNCx601e_AXmJLidt8tBfdzwa3gyXjfXKl4otMeNY93PADqdfWHIQa47QUuCdKapZt6bG9m3UB9BmzEvG1IgS04YLgRr9wJ8XaGJaZRe8BM-kdc4TVjvr9dEN9rHwiIU20xGuxS9sOq3VrcCVRU4lpIOHPNef8zPvW6KRMhyINhsM7Oh-ZETkDKSgW37CS0D1xklzzlbGK_4Ni1_ZGFtZcKh3oGLnhtIZmiiIzGvGgcdhdxGU0X7eECtipd3MOu73C2s9LDJ14GL4Rz8nD0fe_f1M-WSsNVG9q_n2tYXASxghNKAe-flynmUB__PBpnHqmLKoVRbP6YWQVY-t9lhaKEUlHyQt3PKiOHsOXXE0Jsu2txpJqjKcNyOO7HxcXB_PkZQT9Huc5mfJn1RmdSNoQqAljekpvczLkz6eCZvQ1TbeUsPGJtiUUoQ4vxlonp_pq4GKkA8XHZ1--8rS-p1xXeRP8-ty6hANX2mD-sW1Q3e1f8X3F2rr4f/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h31/h001.-5U7kF1wNp3O4e_csF8bT2bzZSa8Bb3kiYd1lcUrIiY) subjects, including specific information on indictments of police officers or for violations of election code.

The new Chapter 56 cites a [1985 statute](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.dGpFhb-JkjYFYhTtQ9l5jKipktJW-LPkppxwe8cG2KmSG9cj5d0r_aYG3qBm4llSQjiZZzgcE70i5yrHbMvPofFN8Vu6EIFhG5fmXgY7KDYZkR8jHXibrOdxvD-fL8CGfTd6N-YuPZn_Vk34LWY6_5Mg-WKywD9NpRUAdQfPYCDoIWSK-d_UAiRTOx4RLdxnKRLp5t0qPxBgfB3T06vn4IW9XdMATp3IXT3OwzNZJuJboNKWsnXS3szk6SALhSquoVEf1L8OowPCd_fun4WoAb8fqfa67TToXIXuMaBVuaS2rsLBrrOHdrtN_hOiLZFKJ5yBlSKgysC3r1ibhQC0mQQnXl4HOUAiRUYGAGXW5aTQSUYHKt-6CxK6OnwLlSAI62mDivo5ZUjjtuTrpVe4b2aWFsKi8x_rFbR_JcDHQWMH6Yo0NsmdJlDYqDM12IH76LKgh8Bhc3BK7AdeO_qlfKqx3ZTzl8mH1LYBblJj4ZE9WwzhAjSJgxMGvOB9Xf821VAMWOmytwBADwx82agDKWih7wGAzNstN1imyOsJed2zwA5NOO0XIZVcuGo32wxEfDyFzSTLbmktQ2CrfTFD7Q/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h32/h001.iapvN57-Oqx63bU_kR4gd8nkTNItW3whCSfM-u4YFiU) prompting district and county attorneys to report information to the attorney general “in the form that the attorney general directs.” To enforce the collection of documents and communication, the rule would create an “oversight advisory committee” composed of employees from the attorney general’s office. The committee would be able to request entire case files from district attorneys at their discretion. Failing to provide the requested documentation to the advisory committee would result in “official misconduct” under the rule, allowing a district judge to remove a district attorney from office.

**Why the district attorneys sued:** The two lawsuits both claim the law cited by the new Chapter 56 does not provide Paxton’s office with the sweeping jurisdiction the rule creates — and that providing the information requested would be both expensive and illegal. One lawsuit from Dallas, Harris and Bexar county attorneys claims the rule seeks to achieve a “political objective” by burdening officials and creating strict consequences for noncompliance.

“These reporting requirements do not make communities safer,” Bexar County District Attorney**Joe Gonzales** said. “They do not identify trends, improve transparency, or enhance public trust. Instead, they create barriers that divert limited resources away from what matters most, which is prosecuting violent offenders and protecting our community.”

The trio’s lawsuit also maintains the rule violates the Texas Constitution’s protections on separation of powers because the attorney general has “no authority” to expand the definition of official misconduct.

The second lawsuit filed by the district attorneys from El Paso and Travis counties marks similar issues with the new rule, and also claims it would require illegally forfeiting the private information of victims working with their offices. In a press release from March, Paxton’s office states the new rule will help “assist citizens” in judging prosecutors’ performance, which attorneys in the second suit worry indicates private information could be shared with the public.

“The Challenged Rules purport to require an unprecedented level of disclosure of privileged and confidential information from only some of the State’s prosecutors for the sole purpose of unconstitutional oversight,” the lawsuit reads.

**What Paxton says:** The attorney general has lauded the new rule as a way to help the public better understand how their local prosecutors are operating, and create consequences for those who do not act. In a statement to the Texas Tribune about the lawsuit, Paxton called the rule a “straightforward, common-sense measure” that aims to shed light on attorneys who may be refusing to prosecute dangerous crimes.

"It is no surprise that rogue DAs who would rather turn violent criminals loose on the streets than do their jobs are afraid of transparency and accountability,” Paxton said in a statement about the lawsuit from Dallas, Harris and Bexar county officials. “This lawsuit is meritless and merely a sad, desperate attempt to conceal information from the public they were sworn to protect."

Paxton’s office also waived concerns about potentially burdensome time or financial costs, stating in the Texas Register that their assessment finds “minimal, if any, fiscal impact.”" [Texas Tribune](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eCyDm7YZItPaA-BvJlf4tsCp9gPKRlKnYLuSTx7ymHCb3neq1mk9VHdKPQRk0z82g0XUGnMHsFkIHF23VtKu-0nDbHfmxKrUaQjx5pzfMWw-GUWeTBJ-fLfiDgX9vgbm0u3yZggIFS3LV2T2RO2RnGvJv8DhCdSD_Y82-JwhluD7gA5wq-EQdjFWp2mUMQ3EXPZ6_z6kFYi2B81JQ7MeaJM35DP0yHNKbnOLCMpXFUT1uPDPs2q-GqyaNAIB5AB1gCGCPsnuxyKY0xr-_v1_F7q2TjEt0hAl-NBSDq5KptpSz58BZx94P_21s3UC7b_e150uxMHkVIs3DSuYcJRfAreJpTDBrJKKbbeqK9vLPaYwNb3KHridSqCn30Ucmy2htZjENx2RMhkcT_zeA02akUUClVcYfvXFCTVIC-v2ZuAzCyB18-m26aelKZeKfeQQ5lQQdTHAOVbWn35FU2VgEkyGHvn7qORbve3LwkkVLOPJI4E--rR0_2B6UIGqbmLYPF8kGRLoLe69Kt4WSr4UWIsGkfKGqfEYB_cZVJuCwQnN/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h33/h001.VRhALqsZjgmN3XbF3Ynuh4DIMIC9zkZZHN2jRvlM47w)

* **TEXANS IN DC**

**“Austin Republican Chip Roy helps block Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill,** ” Dallas Morning News' **Joseph Morton** — "U.S. Rep. **Chip Roy** , R-Austin, is back in a familiar position as he and fellow [hardline conservative House members](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kt4zZ7BA9X6VHUBegwwAun0evC_1zv1YmTsKDRhJ9t1H1SpcxtT6cV-U-00oQsmKP3HQUsbjyFIzc9xsjo0P35HqBliae3p9pE1qyEin1rYVQTVSxZ2Mn3giO66_BOhYgHC7KixNBJCp7WSklXDl_-GFsrXPxP8MNDFwDoyVf6Be8BqHx0Ota-cip10n5WcHvCVKWS67KsNQ79Bf7dkVYPFuYsxX7CKgbJhW67IQvikl6QuoWZJOLoo4Pei_nrDPR0hKYC3tygjyMpIGssdo6-blkvqsnQK8eIoJrutMl6EBcMJaWq6ER3FGZ9znL-UEqLX3iN0XstrRBBnZ59pw364XrnKyiNyfb4KQIEoA1NBbQymx36Hh0NkvKXEqOwbJXSwztKPPlJqKzuEJ1gxCuTVPtAcu9AeR5FX0YdHfCvFT35WyAEEtIgnxWJv8bVXKjG-77-IRwnFm45k7ooPZOlfLz8AqNY0zMqdBN01yk66aX5fti8egxqBKTcRcfSyT9S8EIbEwmtXrqioPTPMhC8dzn2jYWNAfns4IEErITtQSYytigPw4hPBn_rYvt1N6M/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h34/h001.6Pxgy6RD-Gow5qfpkXJUdg1FfbHmuEUMcsxsMPu4Hsk) hold up their own party’s agenda to push for more significant spending cuts.

Roy and several like-minded GOP colleagues on the House Budget Committee joined Democrats on Friday to vote down the “One Big Beautiful Bill,” which is the vehicle for policies backed by President **Donald Trump** and Republicans.

Before the vote, Roy defended many of the bill’s provisions against Democratic attacks that it would [throw millions of people off Medicaid](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kt4zZ7BA9X6VHUBegwwAuno8cGaXVgrLg_CC_Ajir-BusjYjPtQ-j9oGhzKKhYboq_qcv5hx-_Il3Sh_1Cm85MgN5MjZHIsVSeBJnEOdpWXG5rEjPvb5_4y8_9ZxH0R8sqZaERKL5LvQFOcAWAOqmHKVdrwOGO54CFJSgZ9KtNtL0pvxw1hhgA8J1XWOzBTUx2uOljWSqLAMrf-LDIs7YqFWv29UV47QFp3vunKE0r1Y7YUnvUvFh854SCSXOT_EuuYfcH2bPteuxZnDd-ynsd01fHgWPeyHl7KzTzCWgoFdXmzqOEstllO1rOYlvmoeiDUJPMdeMlPceZNpE8EbqStnc5zElbZ3oZJTcw42jYCIXsaN7uVJdR6T17MiZ4FrCEuWm-u2RV-ikL36n3E1Ot16ni1H2qzEJyLqozRQJIqrnjGVnYIDyiz087JdpJV4ssc8hY7bM3sgPi8jzNoCHmCPtrQZNwgawx95aB-kKkKpsM3VWlXZhjY58Nurtr96BW4TM7QodAtBu5HKXdCfsZP8Wbr4rTPMmDbGJjxrAnRQDWs2lhxCsBcZU3PGKqL90KnS3z0BdfBU_QEbY9Bs9c/4gk/tRFSdNRRQ3iTMhVM8g540Q/h35/h001.BXubBPtYMnxfdTgNTVY5hSG14Jrji4ZObI6vpqvQYB8) to fund tax cuts for billionaires.

Roy said hard-working Americans would benefit from many of the package’s tax provisions, funding for border security would repair damage caused by former President**Joe Biden’s** administration and more money for defense would boost the military.
He then unloaded on Republicans for going soft, however, when it comes to addressing runaway spending in programs such as Medicaid.

“I have to now admonish my colleagues on this side of the aisle. This bill falls profoundly short,” Roy said. “It does not do what we say it does with respect to deficits.”

He said the bill is structured to front load big deficit increases over the next few years and backload projected spending cuts in the years following.

Overhauling Medicaid would go a long way toward addressing the country’s fiscal woes, he said, but the bill doesn’t do enough on that front. New Medicaid work requirements, for example, wouldn’t kick in until 2029, after Trump leaves office.

Deficit hawks say that delays savings and means the provisions might never be implemented.

“Only in Washington are we expected to bet on the come that in five years, then everything will work, then we will solve the problem,” Roy said. “We have got to change the direction of this town.”

Roy has deep knowledge about how Washington and Congress operate, having worked for Texas’ Republican U.S. Sens. John Cornyn and Ted Cruz.

He has used that former-staffer knowledge to be a thorn in the side of leadership as he advocates for conservative positions and battles what he derides as budget gimmicks that result in the national debt continuing to climb.

It’s also not the first time Roy has found himself at odds with Trump." * **BUSINESS NEWS**

**“SpaceX will launch more rockets from South Texas** ,” Texas Tribune's **Berenice Garcia** — "The Federal Aviation Administration will allow SpaceX to launch rockets in South Texas up to 25 times per year, a significant increase from the five launches the company was previously licensed to complete annually.

The FAA approved some modifications for the Starship Flight 9, the company’s next rocket, stopping short of full approval for a launch, it was announced Thursday.

The news is the latest development for**Elon Musk’s** space company, which has used the Rio Grande Valley beach to test its rockets since 2013.

Earlier this month, residents near the SpaceX launch site — largely SpaceX employees — voted to incorporate their neighborhoods into a new city, Starbase. Cameron County is expected to sign the order to officially incorporate the city on Tuesday.

Musk is also expected to be in South Texas next week.

"I will give a company talk explaining the Mars game plan in Starbase, Texas, that will also be live-streamed on X," Musk wrote on his social media platform.

SpaceX cannot move forward with its ninth test flight until the FAA allows it to resume test flights following the mishap of Starship Flight 8 on March 6. The launch test ended with the destruction of the starship vehicle about 10 minutes after launch. An investigation into the mishap is ongoing.

Because of that mishap, the FAA is expanding the aircraft and maritime hazard areas in the U.S. and other countries. The safety measure is also because SpaceX will be reusing a previously launched Super Heavy booster rocket for the first time.

Last week, the FAA paved the way for an increase in launches, finding in an environmental assessment that there would be no significant environmental impacts caused by allowing SpaceX to increase launches at their Boca Chica site from five to 25 times per year." **“Taiwanese GlobalWafers opens $3.5 billion factory in Sherman, Texas** ,” via AP — "Taiwanese silicon wafer manufacturer GlobalWafers said Friday it would invest an additional $4 billion in the United States to meet growing customer demand there, as it officially opened its new $3.5 billion wafer facility in Texas.

“Our U.S. customers appear to have a very strong demand for U.S.-based production capacity,” **Doris Hsu** , chairperson of GlobalWafers, a TSMC supplier, told reporters.

“Although tariffs (on semiconductors) have not been finalized...(U.S.) customers are hoping to secure local supply to reduce the potential uncertainties that tariffs may bring,” Hsu said.

The plant, GlobalWafers’ most advanced fully integrated silicon wafer facility, is the first of its kind built in the U.S. in more than two decades and is currently the only advanced wafer manufacturing site in the country.

Silicon wafers are critical components in chip manufacturing and larger wafers are widely used in advanced chip production as they allow for more chips to be produced per each wafer, increasing cost savings.

At the opening ceremony held in Sherman, Texas, on Thursday, the company said it intends to add two more phases to the site to boost production capacity. It currently has one phase completed for production and a second phase is planned for construction in the 142-acre campus that is designed to accommodate up to six phases.

Hsu said there is no set timeline for the additional two phases, but several conditions must be met before moving forward.

“Phases one and two must be profitable, and we need to secure customers... who show strong interest in local production and are willing to sign long-term contracts,” Hsu said.

“We also need reasonable pricing, prepayments, and government support. If these conditions are met, we’ll move ahead.”

The plant is part of the Biden administration-era CHIPS for America program and the company is set to receive $406 million in U.S. government grants for its projects in Texas and Missouri.

Hsu said it has not yet received the U.S. funding but has reached key milestones and submitted the required documentation. Hsu expected the funds to be disbursed in the first half of this year." Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . | TOP NEWS "Could a $6 billion plan solve some of Texas ' water supply problems?” San Antonio Express-News' Lit Teitz \-- "As fast-growing cities, utilities and businesses clamor for more water in drought-stricken Central Texas, an ambitious $6 billion plan is emerging to meet some of their demand. The Guadalupe-Blanco River Authority wants to build a new reservoir to hold water from the river, and construct more than 250 miles of pipeline to move that water throughout the river basin, which runs from Kerr County to the Gulf Coast. Dubbed the “WaterSecure Project,” officials say the plan would primarily rely on existing water rights that the authority already has for surface water from Guadalupe River, but it could potentially incorporate aquifer storage and brackish water desalination. » If it becomes a reality, the first water from the project would be delivered in 2033, with more available by the 2040s as the project is expanded, the river authority said. It expects utilities throughout the region to participate, purchasing water and paying back bonds issued for the project. The project could involve a new reservoir in Calhoun County, a new treatment plant, new desalination plant and underground storage wells in Gonzales County, and pipelines running all the way from the new reservoir to Kendall, Comal and Hays counties, according to regional water planning documents. » It would be the latest large infrastructure project aiming to move water around the region, and likely the most expensive to date, with an estimated price tag more than double the cost of San Antonio’s Vista Ridge pipeline. In recent years, the Guadalupe-Blanco River Authority and other utilities have also spent hundreds of millions of dollars building their own pipelines to bring in groundwater from the Carrizo Aquifer east of Interstate 35 to shore up their supplies, but the WaterSecure project would be the first large-scale system aimed at transporting surface water throughout Central Texas. “We’ve consistently seen explosive growth in this region,” saidCharlie Hickman , the river authority’s executive manager of engineering. “That drives a need for new water supply for the region, and our WaterSecure is a regional project that will help fill in a portion of the future projected demands.”" ($) | PLEASE NOTE : You are receiving the PAID subscriber version of Must Read Texas FOR TODAY ONLY — so you can see what you’re missing. » Become a PAID subscriber here: . | #TXLEGE "Texas House advances bill requiring A/C in prisons; proposal 's fate uncertain in Senate” Austin American-Statesman's Bayliss Wagner \-- "The Texas House overwhelmingly passed legislation Thursday night to require air conditioning across the state’s prison system, marking the third time in five years that the lower chamber has approved such a measure. The Senate has declined to take it up in the previous two sessions. The 89-43 vote comes less than two months after an Austin federal judge and warned lawmakers that he expects to order the state to install permanent air-conditioning systemwide. It also took place hours before a midnight cutoff for the House to pass bills originating in that chamber. is expected to face an uphill battle in the more conservative state Senate, which has repeatedly declined to hear similar proposals. Democratic state Rep.Terry Canale s of Edinburg, a criminal defense attorney, said he filed the bill because the constant, sweltering heat in lockups is “inhumane” and constitutes cruel and unusual punishment. “Many people are not violent offenders,” he told the American-Statesman. “They surely didn't get sentenced to death. But we're killing them. We're cooking people.” Over 60% of Texas inmates are being held on violent offenses, 15% on drug-related offenses and 9% on property offenses. The average prisoner is 41 years old, Texas Department of Criminal Justice Director Bryan Collier has in three inmates’ deaths from multiple causes in 2023. also continue to fall ill from heat exposure, and the system struggled with a , one of the hottest years in recent Texas history. HB 3006 outlines that would require TDCJ to install climate control in one-third of its facilities by 2028, another one-third by 2030 and the final third of the facilities by 2032 — but only if it receives the funds to do so. The agency would be mandated to solicit competitive bids from private contractors to complete the project, and the cost would be capped at $100 million per phase. Canales described visiting the minimum-security prison in his hometown on a broiling Texas summer day, where the odor of male sweat is so strong that “you can taste it” and where prisoners flood their cells with toilet water, then “take turns lying in it” overnight to cool down. In a yearslong legal battle over the climate control in Texas prisons, plaintiffs are asking the court to require the state to maintain cell temperatures between 65 and 85 degrees, a similar range as and federal prisons are required to maintain. Nearly 70% of cells in the state prison system lack air conditioning, in the lawsuit. On the state's current trajectory, it would take at least 25 years to ensure all cells are climate-controlled, which is "insufficient under the Eight Amendment," U.S. District Judge Robert Pitman found in his March ruling on a request for a preliminary injunction. Pitman urged Collier to prepare for an adverse final ruling that would require Texas to air-condition all cells, and he recently scheduled a jury trial for March 2026. nearly 48,000 "cool beds" in its system so far and is in the process of procuring 12,000 more. The agency received $85 million for additional air conditioning installation in 2023 and is requesting another $118 million for the next bieennium, which it says would allow for 16,000 more air-conditioned beds. That would bring the total number to 78,000 in a system that housed in 2023. Even if a bill requiring A/C does not pass the Senate, it is likely that the prison system will receive more state funds to install air conditioning. State Sen. Joan Huffman , R-Houston, that the state budget will include the $118 million the TDCJ has requested to install around 11,000 additional cool beds over the next two years. The state will also allocate $3 million for new dorms, which would be air conditioned, she wrote in the statement, the Tribune reported. Huffman did not respond to Statesman requests for comment." ($) | STATE GOVERNMENT "Texas foster care lawsuit appealed to Supreme Court ,” Texas Tribune's Eleanor Klibanoff \-- "A long-running lawsuit against Texas’ foster care system may get another look after lawyers representing children in state care asked the U.S. Supreme Court to intervene. The petition, filed Monday, asks the high court to review from the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals that overturned a contempt order against the state and removed the judge who had overseen the case to this point. U.S. District JudgeJanis Jack was removed from the case in October, after more than 13 years as the state’s . She issued the original ruling in the 2011 lawsuit that found kids were leaving state care more damaged than when they entered, after being “shuttled throughout a system where rape, abuse, psychotropic medication, and instability are the norm.” She placed the state system under federal oversight, which the state fought in court for many years. In the years since that initial ruling, Jack found the state in contempt three times for failing to comply with court ordered reforms. The most recent contempt order was in April 2024, when she ordered the state to pay $100,000 a day until it could prove it was properly investigating abuse and neglect allegations among the most disabled children in state care. The state appealed that ruling. In October, the 5th Circuit Jack’s contempt order, ruling that it was criminal, not civil in nature, and ordered her removed from the case. Jack has been replaced by U.S. Chief District Judge Randy Crane." "Ken Paxton hit with two lawsuits over county oversight rule ,” Texas Tribune's Ayden Runnels \-- "Five Texas district attorneys are suing Attorney General Ken Paxton in two separate lawsuits filed Friday over new rules that would give the state’s top law enforcer meticulous access to their office’s records. The two lawsuits, filed in Travis County District Court, seek to overturn a new rule created by Paxton’s office giving the attorney general office’s employees discretion to request almost all documents from cases county officials work on, regardless of whether they are being pursued. The district attorneys suing Paxton said the rule is an unconstitutional overreach that would needlessly burden offices who would have to present “terabytes” of data to the attorney general. The rule, which took effect in April, only applies to counties with 400,000 residents or more — a threshold only 13 counties in the state meets. Paxton’s office has marked the provision as a way to “rein in rogue district attorneys” refusing to uphold the law. District attorneys from Travis and El Paso counties filed one suit, while district attorneys from Harris, Dallas and Bexar counties filed another. Both seek to block Paxton from being able to enforce the rule, alleging it violates the state constitution and federal law. The background: The rule, known as Chapter 56, was originally proposed in the administrative code in September 2024. It requires district attorneys to provide all documents or communications produced or received by district attorneys’ offices, including confidential information. Included in the rule’s definition of “case file” materials eligible for review are all documents, correspondence and handwritten notes relevant to a case. It also requires counties to submit quarterly reports to the attorney general on subjects, including specific information on indictments of police officers or for violations of election code. The new Chapter 56 cites a prompting district and county attorneys to report information to the attorney general “in the form that the attorney general directs.” To enforce the collection of documents and communication, the rule would create an “oversight advisory committee” composed of employees from the attorney general’s office. The committee would be able to request entire case files from district attorneys at their discretion. Failing to provide the requested documentation to the advisory committee would result in “official misconduct” under the rule, allowing a district judge to remove a district attorney from office. Why the district attorneys sued: The two lawsuits both claim the law cited by the new Chapter 56 does not provide Paxton’s office with the sweeping jurisdiction the rule creates — and that providing the information requested would be both expensive and illegal. One lawsuit from Dallas, Harris and Bexar county attorneys claims the rule seeks to achieve a “political objective” by burdening officials and creating strict consequences for noncompliance. “These reporting requirements do not make communities safer,” Bexar County District AttorneyJoe Gonzales said. “They do not identify trends, improve transparency, or enhance public trust. Instead, they create barriers that divert limited resources away from what matters most, which is prosecuting violent offenders and protecting our community.” The trio’s lawsuit also maintains the rule violates the Texas Constitution’s protections on separation of powers because the attorney general has “no authority” to expand the definition of official misconduct. The second lawsuit filed by the district attorneys from El Paso and Travis counties marks similar issues with the new rule, and also claims it would require illegally forfeiting the private information of victims working with their offices. In a press release from March, Paxton’s office states the new rule will help “assist citizens” in judging prosecutors’ performance, which attorneys in the second suit worry indicates private information could be shared with the public. “The Challenged Rules purport to require an unprecedented level of disclosure of privileged and confidential information from only some of the State’s prosecutors for the sole purpose of unconstitutional oversight,” the lawsuit reads. What Paxton says: The attorney general has lauded the new rule as a way to help the public better understand how their local prosecutors are operating, and create consequences for those who do not act. In a statement to the Texas Tribune about the lawsuit, Paxton called the rule a “straightforward, common-sense measure” that aims to shed light on attorneys who may be refusing to prosecute dangerous crimes. "It is no surprise that rogue DAs who would rather turn violent criminals loose on the streets than do their jobs are afraid of transparency and accountability,” Paxton said in a statement about the lawsuit from Dallas, Harris and Bexar county officials. “This lawsuit is meritless and merely a sad, desperate attempt to conceal information from the public they were sworn to protect." Paxton’s office also waived concerns about potentially burdensome time or financial costs, stating in the Texas Register that their assessment finds “minimal, if any, fiscal impact.”" | TEXANS IN DC “Austin Republican Chip Roy helps block Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill, ” Dallas Morning News' Joseph Morton — "U.S. Rep. Chip Roy , R-Austin, is back in a familiar position as he and fellow hold up their own party’s agenda to push for more significant spending cuts. Roy and several like-minded GOP colleagues on the House Budget Committee joined Democrats on Friday to vote down the “One Big Beautiful Bill,” which is the vehicle for policies backed by President Donald Trump and Republicans. Before the vote, Roy defended many of the bill’s provisions against Democratic attacks that it would to fund tax cuts for billionaires. Roy said hard-working Americans would benefit from many of the package’s tax provisions, funding for border security would repair damage caused by former PresidentJoe Biden’s administration and more money for defense would boost the military. He then unloaded on Republicans for going soft, however, when it comes to addressing runaway spending in programs such as Medicaid. “I have to now admonish my colleagues on this side of the aisle. This bill falls profoundly short,” Roy said. “It does not do what we say it does with respect to deficits.” He said the bill is structured to front load big deficit increases over the next few years and backload projected spending cuts in the years following. Overhauling Medicaid would go a long way toward addressing the country’s fiscal woes, he said, but the bill doesn’t do enough on that front. New Medicaid work requirements, for example, wouldn’t kick in until 2029, after Trump leaves office. Deficit hawks say that delays savings and means the provisions might never be implemented. “Only in Washington are we expected to bet on the come that in five years, then everything will work, then we will solve the problem,” Roy said. “We have got to change the direction of this town.” Roy has deep knowledge about how Washington and Congress operate, having worked for Texas’ Republican U.S. Sens. John Cornyn and Ted Cruz. He has used that former-staffer knowledge to be a thorn in the side of leadership as he advocates for conservative positions and battles what he derides as budget gimmicks that result in the national debt continuing to climb. It’s also not the first time Roy has found himself at odds with Trump." ($) | BUSINESS NEWS “SpaceX will launch more rockets from South Texas ,” Texas Tribune's Berenice Garcia — "The Federal Aviation Administration will allow SpaceX to launch rockets in South Texas up to 25 times per year, a significant increase from the five launches the company was previously licensed to complete annually. The FAA approved some modifications for the Starship Flight 9, the company’s next rocket, stopping short of full approval for a launch, it was announced Thursday. The news is the latest development forElon Musk’s space company, which has used the Rio Grande Valley beach to test its rockets since 2013. Earlier this month, residents near the SpaceX launch site — largely SpaceX employees — voted to incorporate their neighborhoods into a new city, Starbase. Cameron County is expected to sign the order to officially incorporate the city on Tuesday. Musk is also expected to be in South Texas next week. "I will give a company talk explaining the Mars game plan in Starbase, Texas, that will also be live-streamed on X," Musk wrote on his social media platform. SpaceX cannot move forward with its ninth test flight until the FAA allows it to resume test flights following the mishap of Starship Flight 8 on March 6. The launch test ended with the destruction of the starship vehicle about 10 minutes after launch. An investigation into the mishap is ongoing. Because of that mishap, the FAA is expanding the aircraft and maritime hazard areas in the U.S. and other countries. The safety measure is also because SpaceX will be reusing a previously launched Super Heavy booster rocket for the first time. Last week, the FAA paved the way for an increase in launches, finding in an environmental assessment that there would be no significant environmental impacts caused by allowing SpaceX to increase launches at their Boca Chica site from five to 25 times per year." “Taiwanese GlobalWafers opens $3.5 billion factory in Sherman, Texas ,” via AP — "Taiwanese silicon wafer manufacturer GlobalWafers said Friday it would invest an additional $4 billion in the United States to meet growing customer demand there, as it officially opened its new $3.5 billion wafer facility in Texas. “Our U.S. customers appear to have a very strong demand for U.S.-based production capacity,” Doris Hsu , chairperson of GlobalWafers, a TSMC supplier, told reporters. “Although tariffs (on semiconductors) have not been finalized...(U.S.) customers are hoping to secure local supply to reduce the potential uncertainties that tariffs may bring,” Hsu said. The plant, GlobalWafers’ most advanced fully integrated silicon wafer facility, is the first of its kind built in the U.S. in more than two decades and is currently the only advanced wafer manufacturing site in the country. Silicon wafers are critical components in chip manufacturing and larger wafers are widely used in advanced chip production as they allow for more chips to be produced per each wafer, increasing cost savings. At the opening ceremony held in Sherman, Texas, on Thursday, the company said it intends to add two more phases to the site to boost production capacity. It currently has one phase completed for production and a second phase is planned for construction in the 142-acre campus that is designed to accommodate up to six phases. Hsu said there is no set timeline for the additional two phases, but several conditions must be met before moving forward. “Phases one and two must be profitable, and we need to secure customers... who show strong interest in local production and are willing to sign long-term contracts,” Hsu said. “We also need reasonable pricing, prepayments, and government support. If these conditions are met, we’ll move ahead.” The plant is part of the Biden administration-era CHIPS for America program and the company is set to receive $406 million in U.S. government grants for its projects in Texas and Missouri. Hsu said it has not yet received the U.S. funding but has reached key milestones and submitted the required documentation. Hsu expected the funds to be disbursed in the first half of this year." | NEWS FROM AROUND THE STATE DMN: "What to know about the Supreme Court arguments in the birthright citizenship case " THE TEXAN: "Bill Decriminalizing Homosexual Conduct Passes Texas House " KXAN: "House approves bill to remove Texas ‘homosexual conduct’ ban " THE TEXAN: "At a Premium: Texas Legislature Mulls Reforms to Cumbersome Insurance Industry " HOU CHRON: "Why Ted Cruz is so worried about Qatar giving Trump a luxury jet " THE TEXAN: "Texas House Approves Enhanced 'Junk Science Writ' Following Roberson Controversy" COMMUNITY IMPACT: "Texas Senate panel unveils amended version of $8B school finance proposal " THE TEXAN: "Abbott Requests Waiver to Exempt 'Junk Food' from SNAP Program" THE TEXAN: "The 40: Farm Bill, Renewables Tax Credit, Investment Accounts, Reconciliation " THE TEXAN: "Weekly Roundup: House Deadline, Lottery Commission Abolition, STAAR Test Eliminated " KXAN: "Abbott asks USDA to prohibit ‘unhealthy foods’ from SNAP benefits " SAEN: "Trump 's 'beautiful' bill spans 1,116 pages. name: Former Navy SEAL Adam Schwarze
subject: On my 18th birthday, I joined the Marines
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
You may or may not remember how close the 2008 Minnesota Senate race was, but here are a few reasons why you should know. | [](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxOTYsZJdFZvyrhLLSsMXb1AXl9-Rzc5mxA8tWnd683amR4oy8J9PO1EGYzgXPm3xNz3r0MUGZhboH4KjuOY2cgFbR1W7vGbk-o0X2kVyVcCKek5s5x0ossTNCG7idll1Wxo9i4Tdw2vCDhHCd610Jv_Y%3D)

---

Fellow Conservative,

My name is Adam Schwarze, and _**I’m a former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps vet, avid hockey player, and lifelong Minnesotan.**_ I know it’s early in the 2026 election cycle and you may not be paying attention to races in their infancy, like mine, still, I’m reaching out to you today to tell you a little about myself, who I am, and to make my case to earn your support in the upcoming battle I’m currently running headfirst into.

I’m running for the U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor, who, if you can believe it, **is politically more radical than the former VP nominee**.
**_
_**_People like the lieutenant governor were leading the violent unrest in Minneapolis in the summer of 2020._**_While the governor, whom the nation embarrassingly became acquainted with, eventually called for law and order in the street; his lieutenant governor was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police Department for discrimination._**

The Minnesota I grew up in was far away from the world these two government officials call home.

[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxObnxNfCFk3bp-yOEVcQ0FxZQVu8EGSZDn9UI5YrVDlSknMFdeBUZhckEiYzaagkuwfSHPyDjDedr-zm1AveBb6A0Q5TgKPeoIyUu_CUX5COKuyr6eJcZHqZ0SKw8EgQ3Er508izZ7MiPCtbaY4Hswgw%3D)

As the son of two working-class parents, I have nothing in common with either my opponent or her boss, former VP candidate and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz.

I grew up playing hockey and joined the military on my 18th birthday in October 2001, less than two months after the horrific attack on New York, Pennsylvania, and Washington, D.C. Unlike Governor Walz, I was proud to deploy to over 70 countries in service to our nation;**I didn’t retire before being shipped out to serve our nation in war. I’ve willingly put myself into harm’s way more times than I can count in order to protect the American people, our shared conservative values, and our democratic way of life.**

I never imagined that I would run for high office, but _**everytime I came home from deployment things in Minnesota and the country seemed to have gotten worse because of the radical left policies.**_ I’m doing this because of the state of our current leadership. Minnesota is currently being led by two radical Democrats who have done their best to push this state as far left as anyone could tolerate.

**Something had to be done. **

With Sen. Tina Smith’s recent retirement announcement, now is a time to leap into the unknown—something I’ve done and found very fulfilling. While Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential election since 1976, we’re not a deep blue state.

This election isn’t going to be an easy one for a Republican to win, but throughout my life and career I have always defied the odds. _**And as a former Navy SEAL and Marine Corps veteran, I excel in harsh environments - on and off the battlefield.**_

**[You don’t know me, and you have no real reason to make a financial contribution to my Senate campaign other than that I’m humbly asking you to do so.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxOeQDYkyas6UJLwELOsxh0CSg7SoeksxLZOAJcLcjY02tndYw9F35TrMlVHNY1SEf3gkOgTLaGPpANTn48z3u_2rYZHysBoCeDNlRwoDmhaCWCQf8uAVXw4Q37V0xkVokCyMtFAGQYceYJDHUwEVg7gA%3D)**

[**HELP ME GET MY CAMPAIGN OFF THE GROUND WITH A GIFT OF $5 OR MORE**](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxOe6EYSUdNaS9kqld_G5snaQrSn7aKxfmhcUUxT0gIWhytA4E3HgqNYziy_e85kv-yaWMQnJeNmCwB8LTU5-SOlTmes-Fyuz_AX8G4KEEfclVUxEY_jGRUL31LBhKki8ucrSQmjSV5XgZhhLwRU1yeIk%3D)
_**I love our country, so on my 18th birthday in October 2001, I enlisted in the United States Marine Corps with a deep desire to serve my nation, make my parents proud, and defend freedoms.**_

Twenty-four years later, I’m still looking to serve Minnesotans in service to our nation - that's why I'm running for the United States Senate.

[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxOVT3MmH26HuqK3SQkbo0b2yYhFWTuREAmFbcHR4dCfY5_bQMlUcOv5bU3KiyqpFXwqhia_DHU7hp_USEe0HOd3DOTKZ2DZhEQ49Gzq-k6gd6s94f_GeRveUYRJ6WVrEGNEBm5I9YdCkw52lktPnzMAM%3D)

Since 2001, I’ve been deployed nine times to 70 countries to defend our shared conservative American values.

I obtained a Master's degree from Harvard and a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins University in Security and Nuclear Deterrence. I also received my MBA degree.

[**CHIP IN TO SEND A NAVY SEAL TO THE SENATE**](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxOUpPNjLLyuMdkLB4iEOC5HBzD_1CVkxJjKhWqvIEf6cgKXn3FYqS_C3HvQ0JaVDiqswIxJFs9CRnaSmcLix9zzjKcBCqH_82qUe07S2y0ZnX3tnvlHjmwfmbvahnUjt4uG7vP1gAUQoGPTm_guexL2Y%3D)
So when people tell me this will be one of the most challenging things I’ll ever endure, I smile and nod.

The simple truth is that I believe that my purpose in this life is to serve others, and _**[I’m hopeful that if you don’t donate today, you’ll allow me to earn your support in the coming months.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxOdVa_QJ4VCF7v9P5SrNu-2bZFH_JomAHjqt1SwZJu12DRKigtnqvqgp37KcNuYFh9OItpdLLhFUaE6yKmFEzuZvusM-CBZyBsN72xM2w_XLv-xI1bqS9ZSEjnbD7jFfyeE9tRNufl-9nPQewayfRgG8%3D) **_

[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxORXxlIgXy634Q0qcnP-NXxUBwJJHjdK5OPl_ayuKxM5s0fnjYJg2G-_DMwf_ysKrqyBJ4igSHj7fHPYxWF8WDF01Nyn5KXbtB8TVhEb_jI-Ygg7fbuqxQ9KWg2FfQVUeorziWO00k1tuzO5dtaijI-c%3D)

I’m just a regular guy from Minnesota who was asked to do extraordinary things who wants to make my mom proud, serve my country, and do good by the people I meet.

_**[I'd be so grateful if you could chip in $5, $10, or even $50 right now.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=edb4acf30df54403960e0a9da176a415&_e=fNV5WLQBTcDaYGTyslBp2pTaXmXZIyifTafp3WJxnQphPQKaGd9dcL5QD3Y6X2bovdz-Sd6zttqGjtgn7b4KIe-kLUWWDHdCXT7zMHv9cEwlfKvXJOb1ujIywoRMDhSPVuIm6oAcwECx4jTWWsGcuS9pu0wxoM0p0Gbw9VRzmmNzUkF3x5ON3wHrs_ITvNdFzIfuGDDO_68X1Dz1NVCGQcJEMwiCj4N61MpmVth7Btothh5aV7vi0cdVj16yY6qSO-Z75of-s3cV1XaZH2BxOQK7B2XdUWzZO1jJd-Kq6uYbc0CjAXcHKgIqFDYHB_JEhfN8AHvjgTnEEfOcO7Xqfz6ijj54QvIfb9HNYjL4Y2XgonsZSnAXez8a_Kl9dYSwFAhIYyynpJQUnRBB_Nw91LwHe71h9ihiSAEyLkypBdU%3D)**_

With gratitude,
Adam Schwarze
Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota

clean_body: You may or may not remember how close the 2008 Minnesota Senate race was, but here are a few reasons why you should know. | Fellow Conservative, My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps vet, avid hockey player, and lifelong Minnesotan. I know it’s early in the 2026 election cycle and you may not be paying attention to races in their infancy, like mine, still, I’m reaching out to you today to tell you a little about myself, who I am, and to make my case to earn your support in the upcoming battle I’m currently running headfirst into. I’m running for the U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor, who, if you can believe it, is politically more radical than the former VP nominee. People like the lieutenant governor were leading the violent unrest in Minneapolis in the summer of 2020.While the governor, whom the nation embarrassingly became acquainted with, eventually called for law and order in the street; his lieutenant governor was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police Department for discrimination. The Minnesota I grew up in was far away from the world these two government officials call home. As the son of two working-class parents, I have nothing in common with either my opponent or her boss, former VP candidate and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz. I grew up playing hockey and joined the military on my 18th birthday in October 2001, less than two months after the horrific attack on New York, Pennsylvania, and Washington, D.C. Unlike Governor Walz, I was proud to deploy to over 70 countries in service to our nation;I didn’t retire before being shipped out to serve our nation in war. I’ve willingly put myself into harm’s way more times than I can count in order to protect the American people, our shared conservative values, and our democratic way of life. I never imagined that I would run for high office, but everytime I came home from deployment things in Minnesota and the country seemed to have gotten worse because of the radical left policies. I’m doing this because of the state of our current leadership. Minnesota is currently being led by two radical Democrats who have done their best to push this state as far left as anyone could tolerate. Something had to be done. With Sen. Tina Smith’s recent retirement announcement, now is a time to leap into the unknown—something I’ve done and found very fulfilling. While Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential election since 1976, we’re not a deep blue state. This election isn’t going to be an easy one for a Republican to win, but throughout my life and career I have always defied the odds. And as a former Navy SEAL and Marine Corps veteran, I excel in harsh environments - on and off the battlefield. I love our country, so on my 18th birthday in October 2001, I enlisted in the United States Marine Corps with a deep desire to serve my nation, make my parents proud, and defend freedoms. Twenty-four years later, I’m still looking to serve Minnesotans in service to our nation - that's why I'm running for the United States Senate. Since 2001, I’ve been deployed nine times to 70 countries to defend our shared conservative American values. I obtained a Master's degree from Harvard and a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins University in Security and Nuclear Deterrence. I also received my MBA degree. So when people tell me this will be one of the most challenging things I’ll ever endure, I smile and nod. The simple truth is that I believe that my purpose in this life is to serve others, and I’m just a regular guy from Minnesota who was asked to do extraordinary things who wants to make my mom proud, serve my country, and do good by the people I meet. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

name: The babysitter
subject: A binky for the baby
body:
Like a toddler who throws a tantrum anytime their parents tell them “no”, Donald Trump hates judges for the same reason. He fancies himself a dictator who can do whatever he wants. The only thing standing in his way are the adults in the room - our courts. | | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-6SRgxPablpemQdsLMyKsJZaCIb7AGgSQ7mea5ldme4w2yNjmuvoeL6CRhl03M5N5xDOSfQu2KYQTXTe1yKRgr9lQHI5bJ_NI2fxWTehqbfKCju9WE3bQ3r2h89-DrYN6cAhZcCMOmjHoXUzLe0Uz5FlfJ4fvzNyAjL-6pcbwKo5iXgv6XlA74582EVGhW1xkus-g32VOJLj2sSh7QvKqnGiDvl6uirD-aEEJgtH-ardvfvPDdk1WdvTz1q5OYPcZQz0SgxbDwu_Pj9JPsRifw0/4gk/6p7SInneQvyBBMVacnoB9A/h0/h001.Y2ILW54LFRA9KC3p1niw8Olcd6HLBj0u01RovPqLgrg)
---

Peter,

Trump just had _another_ bad day in court. In a 7-2 decision by the Supreme Court, the court extended a freeze that blocks the administration from using an obscure 1798 wartime law to deport Venezuelan migrants with legal status to a notorious El Salvadoran torture prison without due process.
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-6SRgxPablpemQdsLMyKsJaeQthFYkOEA2nrAQp6hFgJ4h1B3indIUvpvblnHn9qK2QhRqSoF6_MwZ1B3_G17lXhjswrE4GoMSv26q0CKLdAC8dQtkn1GWjXVJGW2Pb7z5vTzpWorpbK791hc1-qtpDUpRJq4TDi5ZHAaEzriw5ImR2Tfoey9T9qoo4Lolj_uHU15zXdgLS_E9SxVotBjIDZNQiwgWuW8ITnG7pa_Cx6_7hukITTqjocPZwcGZQTDFRlIzatm057zQnj92duH5E/4gk/6p7SInneQvyBBMVacnoB9A/h1/h001.p0jLeTiOdsxdQ8cwT7psPLClKx6qpVlT33idyvTA-bk)
---
Trump took the news in stride, issuing a statement of respect for our democracy's co-equal branches of government and checks & balances. **_Who are we kidding?_** This was his reaction:
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-6SRgxPablpemQdsLMyKsJbLxVJR7RpyDzSVawvI8x-7Jjbbz2tigVYSyz3WxZLqAzkFRpfMoNW8phI9lMuv92hb5JgBPvP-2xattWOaizpuk7GSd4e_q1jgvxaD9L5hqQvVZRl6ZQL58T7QxqTsbmt3fWaDznwQZs7qAAGTwXXXj6Ly9IzvtYRQFbuZpztVziJXiKWSYNCaYUX1nH_MPehu5_eChdHTdKstlFQBaXpzt6GnyC5mRXq8p3OcLUWA1XsQOChfErqWIPVFBPZOUN4/4gk/6p7SInneQvyBBMVacnoB9A/h2/h001.U8wHuATSIdq21QjQ839fHVat9EoVauDl0mIMKSHYqPY)
---
Like a toddler who throws a tantrum anytime their parents tell them "no", Donald Trump _hates_ judges for the same reason. He fancies himself a dictator who can do whatever he wants, constitution and rule of law be damned. **The only thing standing in his way are the adults in the room - our courts.** That is why Trump and MAGA have launched a war on state courts in key swing states - starting with the Wisconsin Supreme Court race - and now onto the Pennsylvania Supreme Court retention races later this year. **Trump and his billionaire cronies like Elon Musk are dumping millions into the PA retention races in an attempt to install judges who won 't tell Trump "no."** _That 's why PA is where Defend Our Courts will make its first stand_ \- **to protect the Democratic majority on the PA Supreme Court and prevent a MAGA takeover**. If the far-right flips the court, they will side with Trump on key election lawsuits that will suppress Democratic votes in one of the most pivotal swing states in the nation. **[Can you chip in $25 or whatever you can spare to help Defend Our Courts & protect Pennsylvania's Supreme Court majority?](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT-6SRgxPablpemQdsLMyKsJaMzExgBWz-pGoL-MBBmeMsvejmMva9QY96u93C4niSvWZo25CPEhSi5L8Be82UeubKBcJAYUaGMU_FXxQy9l5vu5jlFHAqlV6NodjP--YCMdBwYm0CDJsb2b6t64ias3HCNeYIaWLwG0ugXl-u3O02kLKoiIKGRyyWMt5FenpTla61C0j5xcPX7UC1t32bp3FXTBwfaFRkBLnWlv5Ydn7MYDW7ctJFgZAvKQZ44dj3HSm99wdJkLJeyrodEHjp6FE/4gk/6p7SInneQvyBBMVacnoB9A/h3/h001.AlnjRWCsgjDoDh5sN_JZvaQ8DZsaK7KVOdTxAwG1hN4)

subject: It’s time for Congress to make good on its promise

body:
Peter, The American people have DEMANDED a secure border and an end to the CHAOS that threatens our communities! | | | [](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/sHR7LsJ_nnj9taRBW8jOur5tLYkhxUNeG126VoTNSag-CJYRh_Fjw3rFCyVcz2ua86KiB4OqMWoAiSuoTn_Eo0fG31-eBTMBXVodJAKckg5dHS7uzgYCt0pxAKrChGGV6oJFdH2EUjCOyFPLIjhyqvt8E4LTHEbGXF8ZNNH3kqEWSlDG6SwwrR9IDs5GjBVz4I1DvVTFaAPCCxF9lNWd5g87VTQ53sCVHi09CTbTZfiCClmEImrFLqn2_gOjb_bD5thYVmMEh0XzpcwNtmesZWJStGAdbQzRykcEGktzu1yts-L49if1KeD6MXr2bUcfHknbaF-GUdqhdOsJ0W4_AF9hidErMF6yRNA7mu7qa0Y5OOQktkEwBGv0wiiJoTEEHFcQHGls_jrnZKqfjrYmIZrhh9e8ca-3rHuFMQHtTEq7ms6zq5T7igCg/6KX3Kkohas_N6-QZ3_y1F7-jyx6fgQzh/9)
---
Peter, The American people have **DEMANDED** a secure border and an end to the CHAOS that threatens our communities!

The budget reconciliation process gives Congress a unique opportunity to fund critical priorities like mass deportation and enhanced border enforcement!

**This is our chance to fund more than $62 billion to finish the wall and strengthen border enforcement and more than $69 billion to detain, deport, and investigate illegal aliens already inside the country.**

But the fight won't be easy. Liberal elites and their allies are pushing back, desperate to maintain the status quo. That's why Heritage Action is mobilizing our 2.5 million grassroots activists to pressure Congress and ensure this reconciliation package delivers real border security.

---
__**[__**> > RUSH AN URGENT DONATION TO FIGHT FOR RECONCILIATION <<**__](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/xuvRR7UNeJG2CKorB_JKkO-PEnxZ1mYrhmtVOjNFLWcnqpdex5PpwPmild_Xijx9P5EbaVSqSuxA5WXWI8mVvANocgM5j2sKWaI6zqMqscBqr5rdb03eW2LiE3KpuaRY6_HYiy9Q411Cq0CwN8Gt0JxqRknfOjqtZICyaiBXjCNuqggF34IigP078d8Y0rO7fMmu-8y1rIMgm4LnKgBzjocijXS6hQeVq-ZD52cNgWSuiLWLcUvzp28Pi-CTiDDbTCgR_CBXxRDCFkkDRZYShWqh3sd4_QggXTcf6VJOoY0HcJ1ahFYCW9kO4omNczE4Wxf_-shSaZjAo1_Yekiu9QG98Hws4ocJ8mzq6H9mgtpcTh95AgCUH27YI0_Qeitq6UJMBw8nzRgxhQ2OxKysCn7sV2nLdYc28ubg4GnLnlOG1Ab-JMPRlQxw/BoOceXbpXMs8O63wIeANh_6N1f9AhY6v/9)**__

---
When you make a grassroots donation today, you can help us ensure reconciliation passes and **the Trump Administration receives a necessary funding boost AT THE BORDER.** Your donation would help us fight for:
---
* **$46.5 billion** for border barriers, including 701 miles of border wall, 900 miles of river barriers, 629 miles of secondary barriers, and 141 miles of vehicle and pedestrian barriers
* **$5 billion** for Customs and Border Protection (CBP) facilities
* **$4.1 billion** for hiring additional CBP personnel, including 3,000 new Border Patrol agents
* **$2.7 billion** for border surveillance technology

---
And a donation today would help **the Trump Administration receive a necessary funding boost INSIDE THE BORDER:**
---
* **$45 billion** for detention
* **$14.4 billion** for transportation and removal operations
* **$8 billion** for hiring Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) staff, including a minimum of 10,000 new agents
* **$787 million** to support state and local jurisdictions combating gangs, transnational criminal organizations, and human trafficking
* **$600 million** to the DOJ for immigration-related investigations and prosecutions, including benefits fraud, gang crimes, and child trafficking
* **$500 million** to the DOJ for combating drug trafficking, including fentanyl

---
**Peter, we 're counting on grassroots conservatives like you to stand up and FIGHT to get this reconciliation bill passed. _[_Make an URGENT grassroots donation NOW!_](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/C4KRNgwBZBX6gXQsK3Q8U6Bj5uGwZmO8mZt13Y_Pu3mpmk9ogXnHzjbuzRVvfVflhC9n-OSlaQdrSA7O2Aq3ngTXOQe2FHA4-UPUxNM8UpLBZJTICJJFItc7MPNkCNECydlM6K7jFxH0hsXh2PM_GLfxTpEEwgNjxrFO5ppv3hDR-8sVtfODrIi-NRrAN7hhLrAZ7ZQjmiNJ7_0y9mfTt9sXJr3E2xPb6DqI7msCJSl8XRIFcC9TKp7u9s7I3aXnmLM962t7ikLdwzf5Tgqbwo6Uj-8TgPlHCZc50x4EXcHuCIHFAxzvcylStYpCWJH9pndW8-JeXyJxlWWlSllnHGxULUOb7BiIM2UL0UAvA0zRX1gUW6SZ7hlcMzDJTiMvhkU_OytO32tsLEMrogvkYTLgV3-eUvmXA1L6duTl1wwKqYxh5T3ig7Mf/mF899q9dMgjAYsiic0qaviEZsrir6Pnm/9)_**

clean_body: Peter, The American people have DEMANDED a secure border and an end to the CHAOS that threatens our communities! | | | Peter, The American people have DEMANDED a secure border and an end to the CHAOS that threatens our communities! The budget reconciliation process gives Congress a unique opportunity to fund critical priorities like mass deportation and enhanced border enforcement! This is our chance to fund more than $62 billion to finish the wall and strengthen border enforcement and more than $69 billion to detain, deport, and investigate illegal aliens already inside the country. But the fight won't be easy. Liberal elites and their allies are pushing back, desperate to maintain the status quo. That's why Heritage Action is mobilizing our 2.5 million grassroots activists to pressure Congress and ensure this reconciliation package delivers real border security. When you make a grassroots donation today, you can help us ensure reconciliation passes and the Trump Administration receives a necessary funding boost AT THE BORDER. Your donation would help us fight for: $46.5 billion for border barriers, including 701 miles of border wall, 900 miles of river barriers, 629 miles of secondary barriers, and 141 miles of vehicle and pedestrian barriers $5 billion for Customs and Border Protection (CBP) facilities $4.1 billion for hiring additional CBP personnel, including 3,000 new Border Patrol agents $2.7 billion for border surveillance technology And a donation today would help the Trump Administration receive a necessary funding boost INSIDE THE BORDER: $45 billion for detention $14.4 billion for transportation and removal operations $8 billion for hiring Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) staff, including a minimum of 10,000 new agents $787 million to support state and local jurisdictions combating gangs, transnational criminal organizations, and human trafficking $600 million to the DOJ for immigration-related investigations and prosecutions, including benefits fraud, gang crimes, and child trafficking $500 million to the DOJ for combating drug trafficking, including fentanyl Peter, we 're counting on grassroots conservatives like you to stand up and FIGHT to get this reconciliation bill passed. | | Thank you for your help, Peter. name: Hillsdale College via Liberty Pulse
subject: Don’t let the media rewrite our founding
body:
There’s been a lot of talk lately about the separation of powers—especially as federal judges issue a wave of rulings that seem to defy the will of the people.

| _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/u/bFKFrBfj-NTWRfeyRHwMFbJQ6WAqsAKAKF7OM92_xZ4p1yV6HQ5NvCo5eBPICtLwE6Wlvy8ET6rQseomVWT2PLe7IsAH4Uy-CEo54d5_Kbqp1MXf77V5HPMLIozvrea9fnY4HwlkcUrstHcQXO6Xpu18aiALXPD8t6dxJQTEN1hsY5ZfqcbluKWi/8XhQ-2CCWf4-t4g4SxsjDv68yHXw9kXk/9)_
---
[](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/Y-7ZgzZeAT3jfdeozy_NbTLJPBrICJ5y6YSlVhY-EJTVSqwpvB4yeGTXu4DYdJaw4cfx4x3OVZYoh9FrU_TPCoLfeVJ4l4RkaKPplqcB29I497tbOH4OsqscmqcEfxjVnUPhZi2Ajcma5hPyzjEdUP24n_mWL0bDuYhBb7x8NgYTudhxnNA6OepIfJtPjMxEJ935YxOq4IE2vCB-DsOcqs3kXHMKjgeo_F9EebWJzphFeEb3k3bTkqxUdKpEKuIoQu1KN_nYhFwGEaR8ZGFUOr4xCK2G5q3B_HqzzF6nBKUwsiZzntyWPK_CMx0Hb9vK9q8e1950WqpC9f_Fx4V7chNffP1UmoUC7iygX-HeyWPOoCRgZCo82TQLD6HSXbPoHrxxFA3G7HADGRMObNm-vmu9jolrhNu5KsGrzkCe7aFRCbRXBzWyeZjX4Y9g70lkTndCSf9jwTWXsSz4UDBy2A/VUBCQJjEdsv_v4GuxxE-ewI98OgeRooN/9) Dear Friend, There's been a lot of talk lately about the separation of powers--especially as federal judges issue a wave of rulings that seem to defy the will of the people.

It highlights a pattern we see every few election cycles.

When in power, the far-Left calls the Constitution "outdated" and seeks to overthrow it. When out of power, they claim to uphold it.

But how should defenders of the Constitution, like you, respond to this crisis?

Thankfully, we don't have to guess. The answer lies in _The Federalist Papers_ , written by founding fathers Alexander Hamilton, James Madison, and John Jay to explain and defend the Constitution back in the 1780s.

Thomas Jefferson called them "the best commentary on the principles of government which ever was written."

**[That's why, on June 17, Hillsdale College will launch a brand-new free online course: "The Federalist."](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/TPzogIFPEIuNLFP0VJ-wpcDv2PYRZYPycTzDx5O-tBZscC3iRroKnduw2VRLnuQJpiLEBeTjQKBRYuQ391RuYYe-BPxknk7xXYqu0mkxHRgu56VighyAPn9vCL8yfFgsryygHQeh_-dMFQ5Ru_I1FdF32wGekUw11YEK_dhvGYioIzTu999-kr01tnJ_8LFQ-dbAug0GuHKhn9DPtT3Je7cnwdu1KigATwCguIyIsgZi-n98QAuCzWAQ6jm6LF3LoTzC76Hk9bmXuQBOtKOvNZ65y6jvnYvHAs1cy55Ipr_QcZ_q6Ixv6czgH_vLow2bd7Dt49wMVYZjOWJQDFzueLPAKYo7NCNu0QrlRZQdFy7eCiIczKGBRXw30LMQO67JjcD8nz_9N5VNeTdllewv1YDjxwSbLR_UJKE-fAyeYeuWMSef0XIL8B9M_GTZiVCUplN1uZKDOdaW4ZyAtZ3iTw/V-8H7rXR3dVAj9TVaw4hATZjsOF397w5/9) **

Taught by Hillsdale College faculty, this course will help you understand the Constitution straight from the Founders' own words--exactly what's needed in our divided times.

We're still putting the finishing touches on this great new course, so enrollment won't open to the public until late June. However, you have the chance **to be among the first to sign up.**

Don't let the media and the radical Left rewrite the story of America's founding. Learn the truth for yourself--with **" The Federalist"** as your guide.

**Click here today to be one of the first students:**

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/the-federalist-papers-teaser**](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/klJELOwImsP4514eb-BjQTN0btaZ5qfT-3Jd2qaF2QGqMe3CIF4Z5qfHMA3YAHe8fVvURvSEsz-QKBE-lyvYoMvF8CFwvcC8RfbHLPlAqByFNjnusX6kH8L-bPbaeg7xqS-vILqOXae94YGEZnjOuoLMV-VRDt_EgX5LyqJDjTmhSDSNf_bkqtie6UKYmI5XtxLRsaqP3ZSt1K3BbtSG5fVSZinZHmwywtjN0t33oNR_E7-6WBJm_a0QvTH8xCjPXP-_8xtxr1es9ZD2I0GhIe-5KinuP5mWicuD2E0rwWoP9jiOXUkIWPvuou-9RHhXiiJIkcH9tV52uz2YW2e1toHXwKNWcmntUKU6sj8iRAtNEteslUln6sZwBfed_XRWKVAl8-lIYB_vahxLPBRJcvaZeZPLJ5S8VpQNlCmIbgF1Vq-OH1zSUizegJOUv9bB3-wfzmPyV5_LnUyKLjj2xw/aF3g5irQYydUFGVfsRqMB_Tg2UL8cDWY/9)

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College | [Unsubscribe from messages sponsored by Hillsdale College.](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/u/O12adn9IpUKe_kjkZViaig1LeftWsCqXuDxYCyoiFvBBbCVyGDCNR3RmTdDrogYGGHKqhdK4jwp7JosxfDG-yXrD4BypVUpP_8Tg_Rk9udixiOzaywwC6S44cB9WFQbM2mZ2JHtrLGBRiqE-8sDoU-RmzHRfl7Dj6p3qeJQUXiYaOq-wvk_4_yqYhGnbeqvprblvFqMgf0kKjYv8UpkOJyGhkxKp-Audwg/0nL6tF_zIVqRhbyKcvlZEdp6B8JaKiMd/9) [Unsubscribe from all communications.](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/u/bFKFrBfj-NTWRfeyRHwMFbJQ6WAqsAKAKF7OM92_xZ4p1yV6HQ5NvCo5eBPICtLwE6Wlvy8ET6rQseomVWT2PLe7IsAH4Uy-CEo54d5_Kbqp1MXf77V5HPMLIozvrea9fnY4HwlkcUrstHcQXO6Xpu18aiALXPD8t6dxJQTEN1hsY5ZfqcbluKWi/8XhQ-2CCWf4-t4g4SxsjDv68yHXw9kXk/9)

body:
I don’t say this lightly.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107890185/546160143/1328794144?refcode=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBkNTM4ZTFlLTUyMzMtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_sK7O0LA2sPQYobUPTk5NHOEeA9_JXTjo8v00EdOuAY=&emci=0d780ce3-7232-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=4823105)
---
I don’t usually send emails like this on a Saturday afternoon. But there’s something I need to say, and I hope you’ll hear me out. If you care about our climate and our democracy, then you have to care about who gets elected down the ballot. Because the truth is, so many of the most urgent climate decisions — whether to greenlight a pipeline, invest in clean energy, or protect public land (_or hand it over to Big Oil_) — don’t get made in Washington. They get made in city councils. County commissions. State legislatures. These local and state-level leaders are our first line of defense, and they’re also the next generation of national leadership. If we don’t invest early and often in down ballot races, you can bet that the other side will. That’s why we’re building a firewall to create space for bold, brave, community-rooted climate champions to rise. **And that’s going to take all of us coming together to hit our fundraising goals, especially this one, which is why I’m coming to you for help on a Saturday afternoon.** **[We’re still short of our mid-May fundraising goal — and with just a few hours left before the deadline, I’m counting on our most dedicated JanePAC supporters from Cresco to step up before midnight and help close the gap. Can I count on you to be one of them?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107890186/546160144/2105508926?refcode=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBkNTM4ZTFlLTUyMzMtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_sK7O0LA2sPQYobUPTk5NHOEeA9_JXTjo8v00EdOuAY=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $5**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107890187/546160145/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBkNTM4ZTFlLTUyMzMtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_sK7O0LA2sPQYobUPTk5NHOEeA9_JXTjo8v00EdOuAY=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107890188/546160146/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.0d538e1e-5233-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBkNTM4ZTFlLTUyMzMtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_sK7O0LA2sPQYobUPTk5NHOEeA9_JXTjo8v00EdOuAY=)

clean_body: I don’t say this lightly. | I don’t usually send emails like this on a Saturday afternoon. But there’s something I need to say, and I hope you’ll hear me out. If you care about our climate and our democracy, then you have to care about who gets elected down the ballot. Because the truth is, so many of the most urgent climate decisions — whether to greenlight a pipeline, invest in clean energy, or protect public land (or hand it over to Big Oil) — don’t get made in Washington. They get made in city councils. County commissions. State legislatures. These local and state-level leaders are our first line of defense, and they’re also the next generation of national leadership. If we don’t invest early and often in down ballot races, you can bet that the other side will. That’s why we’re building a firewall to create space for bold, brave, community-rooted climate champions to rise. And that’s going to take all of us coming together to hit our fundraising goals, especially this one, which is why I’m coming to you for help on a Saturday afternoon. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Onward, – Jane Fonda = | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

\---------- Forwarded message from Dr. Raul Ruiz ----------
It's Raul.
Donald Trump promised he wouldn't cut Medicaid. That was February.
Now, just a few months later, he’s throwing his support behind a House Republican budget blueprint that would _slash_ Medicaid by **$800 billion over the next decade.**
This is not what Donald Trump ran on. This is not what he promised the American people.
During the 2024 presidential campaign, he said he’d fight for working families — but at this moment, Donald Trump is siding with billionaires and his extremist allies to gut the lifelines millions of Americans rely on to stay healthy, safe, and secure.
America was duped — **but it’s not too late to change course.**
We have a real chance to stop Trump’s destructive agenda by flipping the House in 2026. Districts like CA-25 will decide the balance of power, and we can’t afford to fall short.
[Will you chip in $10 before Sunday at midnight? My team and I are working to raise $5,000 for our mid-month fundraising goal to power our campaign and help protect Medicaid and Social Security.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107890226/546160302/-1463534552?amount=10&source=SBD_RR_EM_FR_051725_MM2_30DO30DNTL_D2_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGE4ZmYxYWEtNTIzMy1mMDExLWE1ZjEtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=gMOXcwlCTuBzbJYp8MfXcpweqpz02uSCNLukG8wynlI=)

Billionaires and their allies in the Trump administration are working overtime to gut the programs that help all of us live fuller, healthier lives. And with the help of House Republicans, they’re slashing Medicaid, threatening Social Security, and pushing tax cuts for themselves — all at our expense. This is a full-on assault on working families. We can fight back — and we can win — by taking back the House in 2026. Districts like CA-25 will decide the future of this country. But if we fall short, Donald Trump and his extremist allies will keep rigging the system for the rich while the rest of us pay the price. ## Turkish restaurant has licence stripped after immigration probe
A Turkish restaurant in Kent has had its licence stripped after an immigration probe discovered staff members working illegally and being paid only with food and accommodation instead of wages. The Sheppey business, Mems Mezze, has been accused of "exploiting workers" and knowingly hiring people without permission to work in the UK.
